summary: Matched `func_8015DB2C` in `src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c` by replacing the inline `MUST_MATCH` assembly fallback with C. The final expression deliberately keeps a local copy of `lbl_804D3EE0` for the store target while re-reading `lbl_804D3EE0` for the load argument to `func_8000AF74`, which appears to have produced the desired register/codegen shape. The PR resolved issue #593.
what_changed: Removed the `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` inline assembly implementation of `func_8015DB2C`.
Removed the old non-matching C attempt that used `u8* base = &lbl_804D3EE0->x39[arg0];` and assigned through `*base`.
Added a matching C implementation using `struct gmm_x0* gmmthing = lbl_804D3EE0;` and assigning `gmmthing->x39[arg0] = func_8000AF74(lbl_804D3EE0->x39[arg0], 1);`.
Closed/resolved issue #593 according to the PR body.
smart_moves: Used a named local pointer, `gmmthing`, to hold `lbl_804D3EE0` for the store-side address calculation, matching the original assembly pattern where the base pointer plus `arg0` is preserved across the call.
Avoided the previous pointer-to-element form, which the diff notes caused a register swap and an extra useless `addi` in the non-matching attempt.
Kept the function simple and isolated: only `func_8015DB2C` changed, minimizing risk to neighboring game-mode helpers such as `func_8015DB18` and `func_8015DB6C`.
decomp_lessons: For small byte-array update functions, the way a pointer is introduced matters: taking the address of an element can produce different register allocation than keeping a struct/base pointer local.

When matching PowerPC code that loads from a global struct field, calls a helper, then stores back to the same indexed byte, preserving a base pointer local across the call can be enough to replace inline assembly.

Do not assume two syntactically equivalent C forms match equally; `*base = helper(*base, 1)` and `local_struct->field[i] = helper(global_struct->field[i], 1)` can produce materially different codegen.
Removing `MUST_MATCH` assembly after a C match is valuable because it reduces hand-written assembly while keeping the matched function behavior evident in C.
naming_conventions: The matched function retained the placeholder name `func_8015DB2C`; no semantic rename was introduced.
The global struct pointer remained named `lbl_804D3EE0`, indicating this PR focused on matching rather than symbol recovery.
The temporary local was named `gmmthing`, a non-final descriptive placeholder for a `struct gmm_x0*`; evidence for any reviewer-approved naming convention is absent.
The struct type name `struct gmm_x0` and field `x39` follow existing placeholder offset-style naming in this file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a hand-written assembly block containing `mflr`, stack frame setup, `r31` preservation, indexed byte load from offset `0x39`, call to `func_8000AF74`, and byte store back to offset `0x39`.
Matched the call shape `func_8000AF74(value, 1)` where the byte field value is passed as the first argument and constant `1` as the second.
Used an explicit local `struct gmm_x0*` to influence register lifetime and avoid the prior `regswap and extra useless addi` problem noted in the deleted comment.
The old decomp.me scratch reference was `https://decomp.me/scratch/emRDP`, but no further scratch details are present in the slice.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the PR slice.
The PR had one review according to metadata, but no textual review feedback was available.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `func_8015DB2C`, `lbl_804D3EE0`, `struct gmm_x0`, or field `x39` to recover semantic meaning.
Search for other `gmmain_lib.c` functions that update `lbl_804D3EE0->x39[arg0]` or call `func_8000AF74` for shared struct/header context.
Find similar PRs where replacing an element pointer local with a struct/base pointer local fixed register allocation or removed an extra `addi`.
